name: Canada's Wonderland
notes: Leviathan roller coaster at Canada's Wonderland, dramatic coaster shot

name: Six Flags Over Georgia
notes: Goliath B&M hyper coaster at Six Flags Over Georgia, wide shot showing full layout

name: California's Great America
notes: Aerial view of California's Great America by Oleg Alexandrov, high-res 4608x3072

name: Michigan's Adventure
notes: Wide panorama of Michigan's Adventure park, 11000x2000 original resolution

name: Adventureland (New York)
notes: Adventureland from Route 110 in East Farmingdale, New York, wide overview shot

name: Indiana Beach
notes: Hoosier Hurricane at Indiana Beach on Lake Shafer, 640x480 resolution

name: Silverwood Theme Park
notes: Silverwood Theme Park in Athol, Idaho, largest theme park in the Northwest

name: Mt. Olympus Water Theme Park Resort
notes: Mt. Olympus Theme Park entrance in Wisconsin Dells, Greek-themed architecture

name: Six Flags Great Escape and Hurricane Harbor
notes: The Great Escape theme park in Queensbury, New York, 5184x3456 original

name: Fun Spot America Atlanta
notes: ArieForce One roller coaster at Fun Spot America Atlanta, signature 154ft coaster

name: Sportland Pier
notes: Historic Wildwood NJ pier, now mostly shops/games. Library of Congress has historical photos but no freely usable high-res hero images found. Park is largely defunct as a ride pier.

name: Waldameer Water World
notes: Ravine Flyer II roller coaster at Waldameer, the park's signature ride spanning a ravine

name: Beech Bend
notes: Beech Bend Park entrance gate in Bowling Green, Kentucky, 4080x3072 original

name: Dutch Wonderland
notes: Dutch Wonderland entrance with iconic castle facade in Lancaster, PA

name: Idlewild and Soak Zone
notes: No suitable Wikimedia image found for the Idlewild amusement park (Ligonier, PA). Commons files are for a different Idlewild property in Media, PA.

name: Lost Island Theme Park
notes: Relatively new park (opened 2022) in Waterloo, Iowa. Wikimedia Commons category exists but no high-quality hero-suitable images found via search.

name: Sesame Place Philadelphia
notes: Sesame Place front gate entrance in Langhorne, PA, 4000x3000 original

name: Universal Orlando Resort
notes: Universal Studios Florida overview shot, 3888x2500 original resolution

name: 3501 Boardwalk
notes: This is the street address for Morey's Piers in Wildwood, NJ, not a distinct park name. Should use Morey's Piers identity instead.

name: Alley Cats
notes: Alley Cats Entertainment is a bowling/laser tag/arcade entertainment center in Arlington/Hurst, TX. Not a theme park.

name: Bay Beach Amusement Park
notes: Bay Beach Amusement Park panoramic view, municipal park in Green Bay, Wisconsin

name: CJ Barrymore's
notes: CJ Barrymore's is a family entertainment center in Clinton Township, MI with go-karts, bowling, arcade. Not a traditional theme park.

name: Chelyuskintsev Park
notes: Urban forest park in Minsk, Belarus with a small amusement area. No suitable Wikimedia hero image found. Park has a Ferris wheel and small coaster but limited photo coverage.

name: Como Town
notes: Small kiddie amusement park (ages 2-12) adjacent to Como Zoo in Saint Paul, MN. No Wikimedia images found. Very small 4-acre park.

name: Epcot
notes: Spaceship Earth geodesic sphere at Epcot, iconic Disney park landmark, 4288x2848 original

name: Fort Hays Chuckwagon
notes: Fort Hays is an Old West town/dinner show near Rapid City, SD built on the Dances with Wolves film set. Not an amusement/theme park.

name: Funplex Mount Laurel
notes: Family entertainment center in Mount Laurel, NJ with indoor/outdoor rides, waterpark, bowling. No Wikimedia images found.

name: Glen New Hampshire
notes: Glen is a town in New Hampshire, not a park. Story Land theme park is located there but this entry appears to be the town name, not a park.

name: Haunted Trails Burbank
notes: Small Halloween-themed family entertainment center in Burbank, IL since 1975. Mini golf, go karts, batting cages, small rides. No Wikimedia images found.

name: John's Incredible Pizza Company
notes: Pizza buffet restaurant chain with arcade/rides inside. Not a theme park. 9 locations in CA/NV.

name: Lake Winnepesaukah
notes: Classic amusement park in Rossville, GA (near Chattanooga) since 1925. No suitable Wikimedia hero images found via search.

name: Legoland New York Resort
notes: LEGOLAND New York Resort in Goshen, NY, overview of themed lands

name: Memphis Kiddie Park
notes: Historic small kiddie park in Brooklyn, OH since 1952 with 11 rides. No Wikimedia images found. Very small park primarily for children under 50 inches.

name: Nascar Speedpark
notes: NASCAR SpeedPark in Sevierville, Tennessee, go-kart track area

name: Nitro Zone
notes: Indoor entertainment center (go-karts, bowling, trampoline park). Atlanta location is permanently closed. Small Dowagiac, MI location operates. Not a theme park.

name: Parque Diversiones
notes: Amusement park in San Jose, Costa Rica since 1981. No Wikimedia hero images found. Park profits support the National Children's Hospital.

name: Prime Pacific Entertainment
notes: Not a park. Prime Pacific Entertainment is a ride/entertainment services company based in Cleburne, TX that builds and refurbishes rides and food carts.

name: Rides at Adventure Cove
notes: Small amusement ride area within the Columbus Zoo and Aquarium in Powell, Ohio. Opened 2008, renamed from Jungle Jack's Landing in 2020. No Wikimedia hero images found.

name: Scandia Family Fun Center
notes: Chain of Scandinavian-themed family fun centers in California (Sacramento, Rohnert Park, Victorville, Fairfield). Mini golf, go-karts, arcade, bumper boats. No Wikimedia images found.

name: Sonoma TrainTown Railroad
notes: TrainTown miniature railroad and amusement park in Sonoma, California

name: The Great Escape Fun Park
notes: This appears to be a duplicate of Six Flags Great Escape and Hurricane Harbor in Queensbury, NY. See that entry instead.

name: Wildlife World
notes: Wildlife World Zoo, Aquarium & Safari Park in Litchfield Park, AZ. 215-acre zoo with some amusement rides. No Wikimedia hero images found.

name: York's Wild Kingdom
notes: Zoo and amusement park in York Beach, Maine since 1980. 75 animal exhibits, 18 rides. No Wikimedia hero images found.
